economic_notes: Two GBEs accounted for by modified equity method: Marché d'alimentation Manawan inc. (100% owned grocery store) and Société de Télécommunications Atikamekw-Cri S.E.C. (16.67% interest). Société de développement économique de Manawan promotes economic development. Tourisme Manawan operates a tourism site. Centre sportif et culturel de Manawan operates an arena.
notes: Audit is qualified due to non-compliance with SP 3280 (asset retirement obligations) - council did not identify or measure ARO liabilities at April 1, 2022 or March 31, 2023. Financial statements are combined (not consolidated) and include: Fonds d'exploitation, Fonds d'entreprise-Loyer, Fonds d'Habitation, Tourisme Manawan, Centre sportif et culturel de Manawan, and Société de développement économique de Manawan. Two GBEs (Marché d'alimentation Manawan inc. and Société de Télécommunications Atikamekw-Cri S.E.C.) are accounted for using modified equity method. Tangible capital assets total includes both regular TCA (73,293,368) and Housing Fund TCA (4,754,640) = 78,048,008. Total government transfers calculated as ISC (89,056,950) + Other federal (8,310,872) + Provincial (2,538,091) = 99,905,913 before deferred revenue adjustment; net of deferred revenue recognized in year. OSR calculated as total revenue (90,083,669) minus net government transfers recognized. Large increase in revenues and surplus due to deferred revenue dynamics. Prior year corrections of 121,977 recorded. Reserve for drinking water and wastewater of 4,154,604 classified as liability.
warnings: Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
High ISC dependency (98.86%) — operations heavily reliant on federal transfers
